behavior: Dabbling duck; feeds by tipping forward in shallow water and grazing, and only rarely dives.
field_marks: Large, hefty duck with a rounded head and broad, flat bill.
Breeding male: iridescent green head, yellow bill, narrow white neck ring, chestnut-brown breast, and gray body.
Female and juvenile: mottled brown overall with an orange bill marked in black.
Both sexes show a metallic blue wing speculum bordered in white.
Tail is short and blunt; males show a black rear and curled black central tail feathers in breeding plumage.
habitat: Almost any natural or artificial wetland, including marshes, ponds, lakes, rivers, estuaries, and sheltered coastal waters; also uses nearby open fields and grassland.
